tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P1] Create useRunningSubagents hook with color assignment and completion tracking
[background-agent-indicator] [P1] Create RunningAgentIndicator component with static layout
tasks.description:
Create the `RunningAgentIndicator` component that renders the indicator bar with agent count, stats, and placeholder slots for agents. **File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/RunningAgentIndicator.tsx` **Props:** ```typescript import type { RunningAgent } from '../model/use-running-subagents'; interface RunningAgentIndicatorProps { agents: RunningAgent[]; } ``` **Implementation:** 1. Render nothing (return `null`) when `agents` is empty. 2. Wrap the entire bar in `AnimatePresence` from `motion/react` for enter/exit transitions: - Enter: `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 6, maxHeight: 0 }}` → `animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0, maxHeight: 44 }}` - Exit: `exit={{ opacity: 0, maxHeight: 0 }}` - Transition: `duration: 0.35, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1]` 3. Bar styling (from the mockup): ``` rounded-lg bg-[hsl(0_0%_6%)] border border-[hsl(0_0%_15%)] px-2 py-1.5 flex items-center gap-2 ``` 4. Layout: flex row with three sections: - Left: `
` — agent slots (max 4 visible `AgentRunner` components, overflow badge if 5+) - Center: label — `` with `{count} agent{count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} running` - Right: detail — `` with `{totalTools} tools · {maxDurationSeconds}s` 5. Compute aggregate stats: - `totalTools`: sum of all `agent.toolUses ?? 0` - `maxDuration`: `Math.max(...agents.map(a => Math.round((a.durationMs ?? 0) / 1000)))` formatted as `Ns` 6. For Phase 1, render simple colored dots (6x6 rounded-full) as placeholders for each agent instead of the full SVG runner. The `AgentRunner` component will replace these in Phase 2. 7. Add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to the bar container for accessibility. 8. Add TSDoc: `/** Persistent indicator bar showing running background agents below the chat input. */` **Acceptance criteria:** - Renders nothing when agents array is empty (no DOM output) - Shows colored dot placeholders for each agent - Displays count label with correct singular/plural - Shows aggregate tool count and max duration - Bar animates in/out with Motion - Has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`

tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P1] Wire useRunningSubagents through ChatPanel to ChatInputContainer
tasks.description:
Connect the data flow: call `useRunningSubagents` in `ChatPanel`, pass the result to `ChatInputContainer`, and render `RunningAgentIndicator` inside it.

**File 1:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx`

Add import and hook call:

```typescript
import { useRunningSubagents } from '../model/use-running-subagents';
```

After the `useChatSession` destructuring (around line 128), add:

```typescript
const runningAgents = useRunningSubagents(messages);
```

Pass to `ChatInputContainer` (around line 417):

```typescript
<ChatInputContainer
  runningAgents={runningAgents}
  ... existing props
/>
```

**File 2:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatInputContainer.tsx`

Add to the imports:

```typescript
import { RunningAgentIndicator } from './RunningAgentIndicator';
import type { RunningAgent } from '../model/use-running-subagents';
```

Add to `ChatInputContainerProps` interface (after the `presencePulse` prop):

```typescript
/** Running background agents to display in the indicator. */
runningAgents: RunningAgent[];
```

Add `runningAgents` to the destructured props in the function signature.

Insert the indicator between `<ChatInput ... />` and `<ChatStatusSection ... />` in the normal mode JSX (around line 305-307):

```typescript
<ChatInput ... />

{runningAgents.length > 0 && (
  <RunningAgentIndicator agents={runningAgents} />
)}

<ChatStatusSection ... />
```

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `ChatPanel` calls `useRunningSubagents(messages)` and passes result to `ChatInputContainer`
- `ChatInputContainer` accepts `runningAgents` prop and renders `RunningAgentIndicator` between ChatInput and ChatStatusSection
- No TypeScript errors
- Existing ChatInputContainer behavior is unchanged when `runningAgents` is empty
tasks.activeForm: Wiring data flow through ChatPanel to ChatInputContainer

tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P1] Write useRunningSubagents unit tests
[background-agent-indicator] [P2] Create agent-runner.css with all @keyframes for V3D runner
[background-agent-indicator] [P2] Create AgentRunner component with SVG running figure
tasks.description:
Create the `AgentRunner` component that renders an animated SVG running figure for a single agent.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/AgentRunner.tsx`

**Props:**

```typescript
import type { RunningAgent } from '../model/use-running-subagents';

interface AgentRunnerProps {
  agent: RunningAgent;
  index: number; // for staggered animation-delay
}
```

**SVG structure (V3D Compact, 22x24px viewBox):**

The SVG uses `style={{ '--c': agent.color } as React.CSSProperties}` on the root `<svg>` element to set the color custom property.

```tsx
<svg className="h-6 w-[22px] shrink-0" style={{ '--c': agent.color } as React.CSSProperties} viewBox="0 0 22 24">
  <g className="r-all" style={staggerStyle}>
    {/* Head */}
    <circle cx="11" cy="4.5" r="2.8" fill="var(--c)" />
    {/* Eye highlight */}
    <circle cx="12.3" cy="3.7" r="0.6" fill="hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.7)" />
    {/* Body */}
    <ellipse cx="11" cy="10.5" rx="2.5" ry="3.5" fill="var(--c)" />
    {/* Right arm */}
    <g className="r-rua" style={staggerStyle}>
      <line x1="12" y1="9" x2="14" y2="11" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      <g className="r-rfa" style={staggerStyle}>
        <line x1="14" y1="11" x2="14.5" y2="13" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      </g>
    </g>
    {/* Left arm */}
    <g className="r-lua" style={staggerStyle}>
      <line x1="10" y1="9" x2="8" y2="11" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      <g className="r-lfa" style={staggerStyle}>
        <line x1="8" y1="11" x2="7.5" y2="13" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      </g>
    </g>
    {/* Right leg */}
    <g className="r-rt" style={staggerStyle}>
      <line x1="11" y1="14" x2="13" y2="17" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      <g className="r-rs" style={staggerStyle}>
        <line x1="13" y1="17" x2="13.5" y2="21" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      </g>
    </g>
    {/* Left leg */}
    <g className="r-lt" style={staggerStyle}>
      <line x1="11" y1="14" x2="9" y2="17" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      <g className="r-ls" style={staggerStyle}>
        <line x1="9" y1="17" x2="8.5" y2="21" stroke="var(--c)" strokeWidth="1.8" strokeLinecap="round" />
      </g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
```

**Stagger timing:**

```typescript
const staggerStyle = useMemo(
  () => ({ animationDelay: `${index * 0.09}s` }),
  [index]
);
```

**Import the CSS:** `import './agent-runner.css';`

**aria-label:** Add `aria-label={agent.description}` to the root SVG.

For Phase 2, the component renders only the running state SVG. Completion lifecycle (burst, checkmark, exit) will be added in Phase 3.

**Also update `RunningAgentIndicator.tsx`:** Replace the colored-dot placeholders from Phase 1 with `<AgentRunner agent={agent} index={index} />` components.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- SVG renders at 22x24px with correct geometry matching the mockup
- Agent color applied via `--c` CSS custom property
- All limb groups have correct animation class names from `agent-runner.css`
- Staggered `animation-delay` applied to each runner based on `index * 0.09s`
- Runners animate with the running cycle when CSS is loaded

tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P2] Add slot-unfold enter/exit transitions via AnimatePresence
tasks.description:
Add per-agent slot-unfold enter/exit transitions using Motion's `AnimatePresence` in `RunningAgentIndicator`.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/RunningAgentIndicator.tsx`

**Implementation:**

Wrap each `AgentRunner` in a `motion.div` inside an `AnimatePresence`:

```typescript
import { AnimatePresence, motion } from 'motion/react';

// Inside the runner-group div:
<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout">
  {visibleAgents.map((agent, i) => (
    <motion.div
      key={agent.taskId}
      initial={{ width: 0, opacity: 0 }}
      animate={{ width: 22, opacity: 1 }}
      exit={{ width: 0, opacity: 0 }}
      transition={{
        width: { duration: 0.4, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
        opacity: { duration: 0.25 },
      }}
      className="flex shrink-0 items-end overflow-hidden"
    >
      <AgentRunner agent={agent} index={i} />
    </motion.div>
  ))}
</AnimatePresence>
```

**Key details:**
- `mode="popLayout"` allows exiting items to animate out without disrupting the layout flow
- Each slot animates from `width: 0` to `width: 22` (the SVG width) on enter
- Exit mirrors with `width: 0, opacity: 0`
- Enter easing: `[0.16, 1, 0.3, 1]` (snappy overshoot) — matches the mockup's `slot-in` keyframe
- Exit easing uses the same config (Motion handles the reverse)
- `overflow-hidden` on the slot container clips the SVG during width transition
- `shrink-0` prevents flex from compressing the slot

**Acceptance criteria:**
- New agents animate in with width expansion from 0 to 22px
- Completing agents animate out with width collapse
- Remaining agents smoothly reposition when a slot exits
- No layout jumps during transitions

tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P2] Add CSS-only hover tooltip to AgentRunner
tasks.description:
Add a CSS-only hover tooltip above each runner showing agent details. **File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/AgentRunner.tsx` **Implementation:** Wrap the SVG in a relative-positioned container and add a tooltip div: ```tsx
{/* Tooltip — CSS-only, shown on hover */}
{/* Title with colored dot */}
{agent.description}
{/* Meta: tool count + duration */}
{agent.toolUses ?? 0} tool calls · {Math.round((agent.durationMs ?? 0) / 1000)}s
{/* Last tool name */} {agent.lastToolName && (
Last: {agent.lastToolName}
)} {/* Arrow pointing down */}
``` **Key details:** - Uses Tailwind `group`/`group-hover:` for CSS-only hover state — no React state changes on hover - Tooltip positioned absolutely above the runner with an 8px gap - Enter transition: `opacity: 0 → 1` and `translateY(4px) → 0` over 150ms - Arrow is a CSS triangle using border trick - Tooltip data updates reactively as SubagentPart fields change during streaming - `pointer-events-none` prevents the tooltip from interfering with hover detection - Import `cn` from `@/layers/shared/lib` **Acceptance criteria:** - Tooltip appears above the runner on hover with smooth fade/slide - Shows agent description with colored dot - Shows tool count and duration in monospace - Shows last tool name (if present) in dimmed monospace - Tooltip has downward-pointing arrow - No React state changes on hover (CSS-only)

[background-agent-indicator] [P4] Add reduced motion support and accessibility attributes
tasks.description:
Ensure the indicator works correctly with `prefers-reduced-motion` and has proper accessibility attributes.

**File 1:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/agent-runner.css` — Already has the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block from task 2.1. Verify it disables all running animations.

**File 2:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/RunningAgentIndicator.tsx`

Ensure the bar has:
```tsx
<motion.div
  role="status"
  aria-live="polite"
  aria-label={`${agents.length} background agent${agents.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} running`}
  ...
>
```

The Motion `AnimatePresence` already respects the global `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` wrapper (if present in the app shell). If not present, add `reducedMotion="user"` handling:

```typescript
import { useReducedMotion } from 'motion/react';

const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

// When reduced motion is preferred, use instant transitions
const barTransition = prefersReducedMotion
  ? { duration: 0 }
  : { duration: 0.35, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] };
```

**File 3:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/AgentRunner.tsx`

Ensure each runner has `aria-label={agent.description}` on the wrapper div.

When reduced motion is active, the CSS `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule in `agent-runner.css` handles disabling the running animation — the SVG renders as a static standing figure.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` disables all CSS running animations
- Motion enter/exit transitions respect reduced motion preference
- Indicator bar has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`
- Each runner has `aria-label` with agent description
- Screen reader announces agent count changes via the polite live region
tasks.activeForm: Adding reduced motion and accessibility support

tasks.subject: [background-agent-indicator] [P4] Add error state handling with error icon
tasks.description:
Ensure agents with `status === 'error'` show an error icon (X mark) instead of a checkmark during the completion lifecycle.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/AgentRunner.tsx`

This should already be handled by the Phase 3 implementation (task 3.2) which includes the error SVG branch. This task is for verification and any polish needed:

1. **Verify error icon renders:** When `agent.status === 'error'` and phase is `'done'`, the error SVG (X mark in a circle) should render instead of the checkmark. The error SVG:

```tsx
<svg className="check-appear h-6 w-[22px]" viewBox="0 0 22 24">
  <circle cx="11" cy="12" r="7" fill="none" stroke={agent.color} strokeWidth="1.5" opacity="0.3" />
  <line x1="8" y1="9" x2="14" y2="15" stroke={agent.color} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round" />
  <line x1="14" y1="9" x2="8" y2="15" stroke={agent.color} strokeWidth="2" strokeLinecap="round" />
</svg>
```

2. **Verify celebration flow for errors:** Particle burst should still play for errors (same celebration, different final icon). The burst uses the agent's color.

3. **Add a test case** to `RunningAgentIndicator.test.tsx`:

```typescript
it('shows error icon for agents with error status', () => {
  const agents = [makeRunningAgent({ status: 'error' })];
  render(<RunningAgentIndicator agents={agents} />);
  // The error agent should still render (it's in its celebration window)
  // Verify the SVG is present
});
```

4. **Verify tooltip for error agents:** When hovering an error agent during its celebration window, the tooltip should still work (though it's hidden during the celebrating/done phases per task 3.2).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Error agents show X mark icon instead of checkmark after burst
- Error agents still get particle burst animation
- Error agents follow the same timing as completed agents (350ms burst → icon → 1.5s total then exit)
- At least one test covers the error state rendering
